title: Colchester Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 8 killed including pilot Plt Off. Murray L. McPherson

external_narratives.summary: Memorial database entry for Lancaster DV177 at Colchester. The 626 Squadron aircraft from Wickenby crashed on 25 April 1944 at 04:10 after being attacked by a German intruder near Diss, Norfolk, while returning from operations.
key_details: Attacked by German intruder near Diss
Attempted emergency landing at Boxted (USAAF station)
Crashed in flames, now Severalls Business Park site

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museums register entry for the memorial to the crew of Lancaster UM-K2 (DV177) at Colchester, Essex. Commemorates all eight crew killed.
News report on remembrance service for the crew of Lancaster DV177. Memorial unveiled near the crash location on 25 April 2013. Crew included Canadian and British airmen of 626 Squadron.
key_details: Crew included RCAF pilot P/O McPherson and RCAF W/O Cameron
